Used the QR code for registrations for the first time, reported an electric scooter thing and chemicals in the park to the council to collect, a few people came on the day that I did not know they were coming until they showed up, Jess was sharing great resources with everyone on soft plastic, coffee cup, lids recycling etc., Made a circle at the end and discussed what was interesting about what we collected - too windy to get the rubbish out for a survey
We posted the exact location the day before + to stories. 2 people couldn’t find us still (going to mention in earlier reels for next event the exact meeting location)

Slight comms issue, starting location was not properly communicated to social media team, everyone met at a location (luckily not too far) down the road and had to relocate.

My phone died before the event - used Sharlee’s to capture content. Also didn’t grab enough bags this morning before leaving for the day. Noted to always bring two clean up Aus packages.  I didn’t have the flag in a noticeable place even pinning the location on story some people found it hard to find us.  We were located on the pathway exactly where the pin was in our story.
Just the amount of rubbish we found! The people were also absolutely amazing and so lovely.
No as phone is dead and charging, but will when I receive them from sharlee
We didn’t find much rubbish which is cool to see Tasmanians look after there beaches !

We spent 1.5 hours after the event to just chat about conservation events, learning about nature/animal facts, and many have stated that as soon as they were available they went to this events as they have been seeing a lot of the things on social media for a couple of months!

The above number is a guess, don't think I saw the number in writing either (least not from the day we attended. We know the total for the whole project). The location was difficult to get to. Group photo sent to me became a corrupted file. If I see it floating online, will do a download.

Saw 2 sugar gliders, 4 koalas (including a baby), 3 tawny frogmouths (all entered on iNaturalist). Everyone seemed to have fun. We had someone who was deaf and relied on Auslan from their friend which is great to see our events are accessible for everyone!
Amazing turn out and everyone had a great time.
